paper_title: Archaeological and Lipid Analysis of Pottery

methods.description: Standard archaeological methods were used to excavate and document the site, uncovering a sequence of archaeological layers and collecting artefacts.

steps.description: The section was monitored in 2012-2014 through standard archaeological methods (cleaning, photographic documentation, photogrammetry).
During the fieldwork, a sequence of 24 archaeological layers was uncovered and classified into ten lithostratigraphic units.
steps.searchable_summary: classify archaeological layers into lithostratigraphic units

steps.description: During these campaigns, 86 artefacts were collected and documented.

methods.description: Analysis and dating of grass-tempered pottery using thin-section preparation, organic temper study, and radiocarbon dating.

steps.description: Thin-sections of 30 μm thickness were prepared from the grass-tempered pottery.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare thin-sections of pottery for analysis

steps.description: Organic temper was studied in transmitted light under a polarising microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze organic temper using polarising microscope

steps.description: To determine the age of this pottery, we selected 8 pieces of grass-tempered pottery and 1 organic residue from the pottery surface.
steps.searchable_summary: select pottery samples for radiocarbon dating

steps.description: Six pottery sherds were analyzed in the Beta Analytic (USA) using the acid-alkali-acid (AAA) pretreatment.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AAA pretreatment on pottery sherds

steps.description: The measurements were conducted in the NEC accelerator mass spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct AMS measurements on pretreated samples

methods.method_name: Triple Acid Wash Method for Direct Dating
methods.description: A novel triple acid wash method developed for direct dating of organic inclusions in pottery.

steps.description: Samples were crushed in a mortar to a fraction close to fine powder.
steps.searchable_summary: crush pottery samples to fine powder

steps.description: Crushed material was transferred to the PET tube where hydrofluoric acid was carefully added.
steps.searchable_summary: add hydrofluoric acid to powdered samples

steps.description: Centrifugation in ultra-distillate water was repeated two times to homogenise samples.
Carbonates were then removed using hydrochloric acid until a reaction was running.
Removal of small organic molecules using nitric acid in boiling water was conducted.
steps.searchable_summary: remove small organic molecules using nitric acid

steps.description: Samples were slowly dried at room conditions, packed and submitted to the 14 C dating.
steps.searchable_summary: dry and pack samples for radiocarbon dating

methods.description: Analysis of lipid residues in pottery samples to determine their origin and potential contamination.

steps.description: The results of the lipid residue analysis of the three samples selected for 14 C dating are presented.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze lipid residues in pottery samples

steps.description: The recovered lipid residues are dominated by a distribution of saturated fatty acids.
steps.searchable_summary: identify fatty acids in lipid residues

steps.description: The stable carbon isotope values of the two dominating fatty acids indicate a primarily terrestrial origin.
steps.searchable_summary: determine origin of fatty acids using stable carbon isotopes
